data.title: Proactive self-improvement: agents suggest guidance improvements during normal work
data.description:
## Proactive Self-Improvement of Guidance

Activate instructs agents to periodically review conversations and propose enhancements to the guidance system itself. DSO's /dso:retro does post-hoc analysis, but agents don't proactively suggest improvements during normal work.

Add a lightweight "guidance improvement" suggestion mechanism that agents can trigger when they notice recurring friction:
- Agents should be able to record friction observations (e.g., unclear instructions, missing guidance, repeated workarounds) as structured suggestions during normal workflow execution
- Suggestions should be collected without interrupting the current task — written to a suggestions file or ticket comment, not surfaced as blocking prompts
- /dso:retro should consume these suggestions as an input alongside its existing data sources
- A periodic review (or retro phase) synthesizes suggestions into actionable improvements to skills, CLAUDE.md, or agent prompts
- Guard against noise: suggestions should require a minimum friction threshold (e.g., encountered the same issue 2+ times) before being recorded
- Distinguish between project-level friction (belongs in host project docs) and plugin-level friction (belongs in DSO skills/docs)
